fields.description:
## Why

The epic needs a project-owned Pass 1 finder that rejects concrete portability failures without demanding generic portability prose. The fresh-worktree experiment has already proven that the project overlay, prompt loader, and production fan-in mechanism support this extension shape.

## What

Add and activate `project.portability` in the project criteria overlay, author its Pass 1 rubric prompt, and add focused integration tests for prompt resolution, scope routing, and production finding fan-in.

## Scope

In scope:

- `.rebar/criteria_routing.json`
- `.rebar/prompts/plan-review-project-portability.md`
- `tests/unit/test_project_portability_criterion.py`
- Pass 1 discovery for both leaf and container tickets

Out of scope:

- Pass 2 validity or impact logic
- Pass 3 disposition logic
- coaching moves
- live semantic calibration
- remediation of existing portability debt

## Approach

Use the existing project overlay seam rather than adding a built-in criterion. This keeps rebar-specific policy in the rebar repository while exercising the same supported extension boundary available to clients. Configure `exec: 1-TURN`, `facet: project-invariants` (the established free-form project grouping used by `tests/unit/test_criteria_overlay.py`), `applies_at.scope: [container, leaf]`, `default_posture: blocking`, and `block_threshold: 0.9`, then add the id to `activate`.

The prompt front matter is fixed as:

- `schema_version: 1`
- `title: Rebar portability`
- `description: Find concrete rebar portability failures across supported client shapes.`
- `execution_mode: single_turn`, the prompt-library counterpart of routing `exec: 1-TURN`
- `category: plan-review-criterion`
- `dimension: project-invariants`

The rubric body uses these exact second-level headings so tests have a mechanical oracle:

- `## Finding threshold`
- `## Required finding fields`
- `## Supported client-shape matrix`
- `## Non-findings`

Under `Finding threshold`, require all four counterexample elements before emitting a finding: cited plan mechanism, materially different supported client shape, causal failure mechanism, and observable breakage scenario.

Under `Required finding fields`, specify `location: str` for the plan citation, `finding: str` for the assumption plus causal mechanism, `scenarios: list[str]` for the alternate client shape plus observable breakage, `evidence: list[str]` for the plan quote plus grounding facts, and `criteria: list[str]` containing `project.portability`.

Under `Supported client-shape matrix`, include these exact labels and values:

- `Harness`: Python library, CLI, remote MCP; no Claude Code or Codex dependency.
- `Target project`: Ruby, Python, Java, Next.js, .NET, Terraform subprojects in a monorepo.
- `Platform and venue`: macOS, Windows, Linux, BSD, CI, servers, developer workstations.
- `Project location and access`: in-checkout current working directory, explicitly located workspace, server outside the checkout, no unrestricted-local-filesystem assumption.

Under `Non-findings`, require the literal rules `Silence about portability is not a finding` and `Project-specific behavior behind project configuration or an explicit extension boundary is allowed`.

## Grounding

Prior art is `tests/unit/test_criteria_overlay.py`, which proves the overlay and prompt contract, plus `tests/unit/workflow/test_production_batch_runner.py`, which covers the real fan-in runner. The parent epic `jira-reb-1003` records the completed experiment at `2026-07-16T00:50:08Z`: worktree `/Users/joeoakhart/rebar-jira-reb-1003-portability`, branch `experiment/jira-reb-1003-portability`, base `b0ac25447203d864463408c933497f21bc1f8dbf`, result `experiment=PASS; routed_scopes={container:true,leaf:true}; project_fan_in=[project.portability]; surfaced_findings=1; move_loaded=project-portability; restricted_to=project.portability`.

## Test Strategy

Exercise the real overlay loader, prompt resolver, routing registry, and `ProductionBatchRunner` fan-in with deterministic fakes. Tests inspect the authored rubric contract; live LLM behavior belongs to the calibration child.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] The overlay activates `project.portability` with `exec: 1-TURN`, `facet: project-invariants`, and `applies_at.scope: [container, leaf]`. Prove with `.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_project_portability_criterion.py -k criterion_activation`.
- [ ] The activated criterion uses `default_posture: blocking` plus `block_threshold: 0.9`. Prove with `.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_project_portability_criterion.py -k criterion_activation`.
- [ ] The project rubric declares the exact six front-matter values specified in this plan. Prove with `.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_project_portability_criterion.py -k prompt_frontmatter`.
- [ ] The project rubric resolves through the production prompt loader for `project.portability`. Prove with `.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_project_portability_criterion.py -k prompt_resolution`.
- [ ] A leaf plan routes `project.portability`. Prove with `.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_project_portability_criterion.py -k leaf_routing`.
- [ ] A container plan routes `project.portability`. Prove with `.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_project_portability_criterion.py -k container_routing`.
- [ ] Production project fan-in surfaces an injected `project.portability` finding. Prove with `.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_project_portability_criterion.py -k production_fan_in`.
- [ ] The rubric contains the exact `Finding threshold` heading plus all four counterexample elements. Prove with `.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_project_portability_criterion.py -k finding_threshold`.
- [ ] The rubric contains the exact `Required finding fields` heading plus the five typed field markers specified in this plan. Prove with `.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_project_portability_criterion.py -k finding_schema_markers`.
- [ ] The rubric contains every label-value pair specified under `Supported client-shape matrix`. Prove with `.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_project_portability_criterion.py -k client_shape_matrix`.
- [ ] The rubric contains the exact rule `Silence about portability is not a finding`. Prove with `.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_project_portability_criterion.py -k omission_guard`.
- [ ] The rubric contains the exact rule `Project-specific behavior behind project configuration or an explicit extension boundary is allowed`. Prove with `.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_project_portability_criterion.py -k project_configuration_guard`.
- [ ] Existing behavior remains unchanged when the project overlay is absent. Prove with `.venv/bin/pytest -q tests/unit/test_criteria_overlay.py tests/unit/workflow/test_production_batch_runner.py`.
